Ashwitha_FDE_Take_Home_Assignment .pdf CONFIDENTIAL  |  Intelligent Client Onboarding Engine — PRD v1.0 
Ashwitha  |  FDE Take-Home  |  Aiwah Labs  |  Page 1 of 6 
P  R  O  D  U  C  T     R  E  Q  U  I  R  E  M  E  N  T  S     D  O  C  U  M  E  N  T 
 
Intelligent Client Onboarding Engine 
Automating B2B Onboarding from 45 Minutes to Under 3 Minutes 
 
A U T H O R 
Ashwitha 
R O L E 
Forward Deployed 
Engineer 
V E R S I O N 
1.0 
D A T E 
June 2026 
A U D I E N C E 
CTO · Ops Lead · PM 
 
01 · P R O B L E M   S T A T E M E N T 
 
 
A 30-person B2B company relies entirely on manual effort to onboard every new client after a deal closes. One 
employee copies client data from the CRM into four separate systems; a second employee verifies the work. The 
process averages 45 minutes per client, produces frequent data inconsistencies, and provides no centralized visibility 
into onboarding status. 
 
Pain Point Current State Business Impact 
Onboarding duration ~45 min / client Ops team bottleneck; limits monthly 
capacity 
Human verification 2 employees always required 40+ min of senior-staff time per deal 
Data entry errors Frequent; no detection layer Invoice mismatches, wrong email recipients 
Cross-system consistency Manual; varies per employee CRM, invoice & sheet regularly out of sync 
Onboarding visibility None Leadership cannot track status or failures 
 
02 · G O A L S   &   S U C C E S S   M E T R I C S 
 
 
Objective Baseline Target Measurement 
End-to-end onboarding time ~45 min < 3 min Median run time in audit log 
Manual effort per onboarding ~45 min < 3 min Time logged in Command 
Center 
Automated completion rate 0% ≥ 95% % runs requiring no human 
action 
Cross-system data accuracy ~70% est. 99.5% Verification engine 
mismatch rate 
Human reviewers required 2 always 0 (exceptions only) Reviews triggered / total 
runs 
Failed step self-recovery N/A ≥ 80% auto-resolved Retries succeeded / retries 
total 
 
03 · P R O P O S E D   S O L U T I O N 
 
 

CONFIDENTIAL  |  Intelligent Client Onboarding Engine — PRD v1.0 
Ashwitha  |  FDE Take-Home  |  Aiwah Labs  |  Page 2 of 6 
Build an Intelligent Onboarding Engine: a centralized onboarding orchestration platform that listens for a Closed Won 
event in the CRM, validates client data, scores onboarding confidence, executes all downstream steps in parallel, 
verifies each result, and self-heals on failure — all without human involvement for standard cases. A real-time 
Command Center gives Operations full visibility. 
 
0 3 B  · S Y S T E M   A R C H I T E C T U R E 
 
 
INTELLIGENT CLIENT ONBOARDING ENGINE — ARCHITECTURE 
CRM  →  Closed Won Event  →  Webhook Fired 
↓ 
Data Ingestion & Normalization 
↓ 
Validation Engine 
↓ 
Confidence Scoring Engine 
↓ 
Parallel Orchestration Engine 
Invoice Welcome 
Email 
Google Sheet Checklist Notify Team (parallel) 
↓ 
Verification Engine  +  Self-Healing Layer 
↓ 
Onboarding Command Center  →  Complete 
 
 
04 · O P E R A T I O N A L   W O R K F L O W 
 
 
# Stage What Happens SLA 
1 
CRM Trigger 
Webhook fires the instant a deal transitions to Closed Won. 
Payload captured and queued atomically. 
< 1s 
2 
Data Ingestion 
Pull full client record from CRM API. Normalize field names, 
sanitize inputs, snapshot to internal store. 
< 2s 
3 
Validation Engine 
Check all required fields (email, billing, contract). Flag duplicates. 
Detect incomplete contracts or invalid billing data. 
< 3s 
4 
Confidence Scoring 
Score 0–100 based on field completeness, data quality signals, 
and duplicate detection. Route to auto / review / manual lane. 
< 1s 
5 
Parallel Orchestration 
Simultaneously: generate invoice · send welcome email · update 
Google Sheet · create project checklist · notify internal team. 
< 20s 

CONFIDENTIAL  |  Intelligent Client Onboarding Engine — PRD v1.0 
Ashwitha  |  FDE Take-Home  |  Aiwah Labs  |  Page 3 of 6 
6 
Verification Engine 
Confirm each step completed successfully and all records match 
the CRM snapshot. Trigger self-healing on mismatch. 
< 5s 
7 
Self-Healing Layer 
On step failure: retry (×3, exponential backoff) · correct data if 
possible · escalate to ops queue only if unresolvable. 
< 30s 
8 
Command Center 
Update 
Write full audit log. Update live dashboard: status, duration, step 
outcomes, any escalations. Mark onboarding complete. 
< 2s 
 
Total target wall-clock time: < 3 minutes end-to-end (steps 5–6 run in parallel). 
 
05 · C O N F I D E N C E   S C O R I N G   E N G I N E 
 
 
Every onboarding run is assigned a confidence score (0–100) before any action is executed. The score determines the 
routing lane and eliminates blanket human review. 
 
Score Band Confidence Level System Action 
80 – 100 HIGH Fully automated. No human involvement. Onboarding completes in 
< 3 minutes. 
50 – 79 MEDIUM Automated execution proceeds; account owner receives a review 
request before the invoice is finalized. 
0 – 49 LOW Onboarding paused. Ops queue notified with specific failure 
reasons. Man

fullText:
PINIT-DNA_SmartLinks_Audit.pdf 2. Access Policies 
 
Feature Status Evidence 
Secure link generation DONE 
share-link.service.ts — crypto.randomBytes(8) → base64url 
token, uniqueness checked vs DB 
JWT token generation MISSING 
jsonwebtoken is in package.json but never imported/used in 
share code — opaque random tokens used instead 
HMAC signature MISSING 
No crypto.createHmac anywhere in src/services/share/ 
Session nonce PARTIAL 
Client-generated sessionId (sessionStorage) groups events 
— not a cryptographic nonce 
Link expiration DONE 
expiresAt field; expiresIn (hrs) → computed date; isExpired 
check in getPublicInfo() 
One-time access links MISSING 
Only generic maxViews counter — no single-use flag 
Link revocation DONE 
ShareLinkService.revoke() sets isActive=false; DELETE 
/api/v1/share/:token wired 
Download limits PARTIAL 
allowDownload is boolean on/off — no numeric 'max N 
downloads' 
View-only mode DONE 
When allowDownload=false, Download button hidden; file 
streamed inline only 
Files: share-link.service.ts, share-link.controller.ts, share.routes.ts 
Endpoints: POST /api/v1/share, DELETE /api/v1/share/:token 
Database: share_links table 
 
 
Feature Status Evidence 
Access policy builder PARTIAL 
ShareModal in VaultPage.tsx — basic form 
(expiry/views/download/name/note), not a generalized builder 
Expiry policies DONE 
Presets: 1h / 24h / 7 days / 30 days / never 
Device restrictions MISSING 
Device type only recorded, never enforced 
Geo restrictions MISSING 
Country/city only logged, never used to allow/deny 
Email verification MISSING 
No email field/flow in share code 
OTP verification MISSING 
Zero matches in codebase 
MFA escalation MISSING 
Not implemented 
User-bound access MISSING 
requireName captures free-text name only — not an 
authenticated identity 
Files: client/src/pages/VaultPage.tsx (ShareModal)

pointer-events-none

onContextMenu={e => e.preventDefault()} on page wrapper 
Print disable MISSING 
Only DETECTS print attempts (beforeprint + matchMedia) — 
content not hidden from print

Download button conditionally rendered only if 
allowDownload=true 
Blur on inactivity MISSING 
No idle-blur logic anywhere 
Tab-switch detection DONE 
visibilitychange listener → track('TAB_SWITCH') 
Session timeout MISSING

track('VIEWED') on load → ShareAccessLog row +

handleDownload() → track('DOWNLOADED')

Scroll depth tracking DONE 
onScroll computes %, fires SCROLL at 25/50/75/100% 
milestones

sessionId groups events but duration never 
computed/displayed 
Access history DONE 
GET /api/v1/share/:token/logs → full accessLogs list per link 
Audit export MISSING 
No CSV/PDF export specific to share access logs

Endpoints: GET /api/v1/share/:token/logs, GET /api/v1/share/timeline/:dnaId
